title: calamagrostis aurea
intro: Calamagrostis aurea, also known as golden feather grass, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calamagrostis aurea include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Calamagrostis aurea is commonly prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and its dried leaves can also be used in herbal remedies and supplements.
